title: Monopole Argument for Fractional Excitations
summary: If the Hall coefficient were noninteger without additional excitations, a spherical Hall sample around a unit monopole would carry noninteger charge, so fractionally charged quasiparticles must appear; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 1913-1944; eqs. groof, droof).
assumptions: Scope: coarse-grained charge accounting for a spherical Hall sample in the presence of one Dirac monopole quantum.
regime: Promoted from AITP topic witten-topological-phases-1510-07698-full-paper via candidate candidate:monopole-argument-for-fractional-excitations after passing the documented AI coverage and consensus gate.
scope: Is the monopole thought-experiment argument for fractional excitations ready to be stored as a reusable proof_fragment?

source_anchors.notes: AITP promoted candidate candidate:monopole-argument-for-fractional-excitations from topic witten-topological-phases-1510-07698-full-paper; keep upstream auto-adjudication artifacts for full provenance.

math_expressions.label: dfor
math_expressions.context: Charge density predicted by the Chern-Simons response in the integer Hall case.

math_expressions.label: groof
math_expressions.context: Unit Dirac monopole flux piercing the spherical sample.

math_expressions.label: droof
math_expressions.context: Induced electric charge of the Hall material surrounding the monopole.

magnetic flux density through the two-dimensional sample
total induced electric charge of the spherical sample
